verdict_reasoning: B4GALT1 is a type II Golgi-resident glycosyltransferase, but it has a well-documented dual-localization PM pool: a longer isoform with extended cytoplasmic tail traffics to the plasma membrane where it functions as a cell-surface lectin/adhesion molecule, notably on sperm (binding ZP3) and on migrating/metastatic cells, with the catalytic domain exposed extracellularly. This surface pool is real and uses its own type-II TM anchor at the PM. Not lysosomal exocytosis; not cell-state-only; not wash-resistant partner tethering. Cancer ecto-display and sperm-surface display both support contextual; dual_localization best captures the documented Golgi+PM steady-state distribution.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Could alternatively be tissue_restricted_surface given prominent sperm-surface role; dual_localization chosen because PM pool exists more broadly in somatic/tumor cells.
